channel.title: Nature
channel.description: Nature is the foremost international weekly scientific journal in the world and is the flagship journal for Nature Portfolio. It publishes the finest peer-reviewed research in all fields of science and technology on the basis of its originality, importance, interdisciplinary interest, timeliness, accessibility, elegance and surprising conclusions. Nature publishes landmark papers, award winning news, leading comment and expert opinion on important, topical scientific news and events that enable readers to share the latest discoveries in science and evolve the discussion amongst the global scientific community.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: The squeal of peeling tape, explained
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 02 March 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00516-w

Weak shock waves triggered by micro-cracks in the adhesive layer of tape cause it to screech when being unspooled from its roll.
item.dc:title: The squeal of peeling tape, explained

item.title: Reply to: Limitations of probing field-induced response with STM
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10127-0

Reply to: Limitations of probing field-induced response with STM
item.dc:title: Reply to: Limitations of probing field-induced response with STM
item.dc:creator: Yuqing Xing
Seokjin Bae
Stephen D. Wilson
Ziqiang Wang
Rafael M. Fernandes
Vidya Madhavan

item.title: The surprising science of squeaky sneakers
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00620-x

The mystery of why soft shoes squeak on hard floors may be solved, according to new research
item.dc:title: The surprising science of squeaky sneakers
item.dc:creator: Dan Fox

item.title: Limitations of probing field-induced response with STM
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10126-1

Limitations of probing field-induced response with STM
item.dc:title: Limitations of probing field-induced response with STM
item.dc:creator: Christopher Candelora
Ilija Zeljkovic

item.title: Cavity-altered superconductivity
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10062-6

A new platform making use of hexagonal boron nitride interfaced with the molecular superconductor κ-(BEDT-TTF)2Cu[N(CN)2]Br is demonstrated for realizing cavity-altered materials, confirmed by magnetic force microscopy and nano-optical measurements.
item.dc:title: Cavity-altered superconductivity
item.dc:creator: Itai Keren
Tatiana A. Webb
Shuai Zhang
Jikai Xu
Dihao Sun
Brian S. Y. Kim
Dongbin Shin
Songtian S. Zhang
Junhe Zhang

Qiang Li
Michael A. Sentef
Angel Rubio

D. N. Basov

item.title: Echinoderm stereom gradient structures enable mechanoelectrical perception
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10164-9

Sea urchin spines show exceptional mechanoelectrical sensing from gradient structures and can be replicated in 3D-printed mimics for underwater sensing.
item.dc:title: Echinoderm stereom gradient structures enable mechanoelectrical perception
item.dc:creator: Annan Chen
Ziqin Wang
Zhizi Guan
Jiajun Wu
Qi Wei Shi
Senlin Wang
Yusheng Shi
Bin Su
Chunze Yan
Zuankai Wang
Jian Lu

item.title: Squeaking at soft–rigid frictional interfaces
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10132-3

High-speed imaging reveals that the squeak of soft–rigid frictional interfaces, like sneakers sliding on a basketball court, arises from intersonic opening slip pulses—analogous to earthquake ruptures—that thin ridges on the rubber confine to repeat at a musical frequency.
item.dc:title: Squeaking at soft–rigid frictional interfaces
item.dc:creator: Adel Djellouli
Gabriele Albertini
Jackson Wilt
Vincent Tournat
David Weitz
Shmuel Rubinstein
Katia Bertoldi

item.title: Pivoting colloidal assemblies exhibit mechanical metamaterial behaviour
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10217-z

A method is described for the manufacture of pivoting colloidal assemblies comprising rotating diamond and rotating triangle geometries that show tunable folding and unfolding by thermal fluctuations and actuation by magnetic attractions.
item.dc:title: Pivoting colloidal assemblies exhibit mechanical metamaterial behaviour
item.dc:creator: Julio Melio
Martin van Hecke
Silke E. Henkes
Daniela J. Kraft

item.title: Field-free full switching of chiral antiferromagnetic order
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10175-6

An Mn3Sn homo-junction enables efficient field-free full switching of chiral antiferromagnets with greatly reduced power consumption, advancing their potential for energy-efficient magnetic memory devices with advantages of ultradense integration and ultrafast speed.
item.dc:title: Field-free full switching of chiral antiferromagnetic order
item.dc:creator: Zhiyuan Zhou
Yanzhang Cao
Zhuorui Pan
Yingying Zhang
Shixuan Liang
Feng Pan
Cheng Song

item.title: Hydrofluorocarbon electrolytes for energy-dense and low-temperature batteries
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10210-6

A method using hydrofluorocarbon electrolytes for synthesizing alkanes with monofluorinated structures is described, yielding a pathway for manufacturing lithium-metal batteries able to achieve high energy density as well as operate at low temperature.
item.dc:title: Hydrofluorocarbon electrolytes for energy-dense and low-temperature batteries
item.dc:creator: Lanqing Wu
Jinyu Zhang
Yong Li
Zhenyu Fan
Shuangxin Ren
Jie Zhang
Yawen Li
Youxuan Ni
Weiwei Xie
Yong Lu
Jun Chen
Qing Zhao

item.title: Uncovering origins of heterogeneous superconductivity in La<sub>3</sub>Ni<sub>2</sub>O<sub>7</sub>
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10095-x

Wide-field quantum sensing shows μm-scale inhomogeneous superconductivity in high-pressure La3Ni2O7, linking local diamagnetic response to stress and stoichiometry and clarifying mechanisms that suppress or enhance superconductivity.
item.dc:title: Uncovering origins of heterogeneous superconductivity in La<sub>3</sub>Ni<sub>2</sub>O<sub>7</sub>
item.dc:creator: S. V. Mandyam
E. Wang
Z. Wang
B. Chen
N. C. Jayarama
A. Gupta
E. A. Riesel
V. I. Levitas
C. R. Laumann
N. Y. Yao

item.title: Entanglement-assisted non-local optical interferometry in a quantum network
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 25 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10171-w

Entangled quantum memories are used in a quantum network of silicon–vacancy centres in diamond nanocavities to experimentally perform non-local phase measurements.
item.dc:title: Entanglement-assisted non-local optical interferometry in a quantum network
item.dc:creator: P.-J. Stas
Y.-C. Wei
M. Sirotin
Y. Q. Huan
U. Yazlar
